pipeline.invariant: tokens, trivia, original spelling, and recovery provenance remain diagnosable
surface sugar is normalized without selecting overload, witness, ownership, or backend representation
unresolved slots are analysis inputs and never serializable canonical output
name, call, label, type, evidence, extension, provider, ownership, effect, error, cancellation, suspension, isolation, authority, and cleanup decisions close before sealing
canonicalization and the fail-closed HIR verifier remove no semantic decision and admit no recovery or unresolved variant
a valid MirCapabilityReceipt for exact MIR schema and lowering-rules digests is mandatory
lowering expands fixed HIR decisions into control, value, place, token, cleanup, outcome, and suspension structure without semantic re-selection

CheckedIntPow.inputs: one exact integer domain plus StaticNonnegativeProofId
CheckedIntPow.failure: checked overflow Defect before enclosing commit

FloatPowInt.inputs: Float32 or Float64 base plus exact signed integer exponent descriptor
FloatPowInt.failure: no recoverable error; special values are ordinary values

FloatPow.inputs: closed Float32 or Float64 adapted domain
FloatPow.failure: no recoverable error; negative finite base and nonintegral exponent follow the bound special-value profile

ComplexPowInt.inputs: Complex64 semantic value plus exact signed integer exponent descriptor
ComplexPowInt.failure: no recoverable error; zero and nonfinite cases follow the bound special-value profile

ComplexPowPrincipal.inputs: Complex64 semantic base and exponent after a closed adaptation
ComplexPowPrincipal.failure: no recoverable error; all zero and nonfinite cases follow the bound special-value profile

RationalConst.semantic_status: canonical HIR representation only; no Rational power route or source activation is created
normalization: integer encodings are shortest unsigned big-endian magnitudes

ComplexLiteral.component_encoding: exact lowercase sixteen-hex-digit IEEE-754 binary64 bit pattern

current_mir_bridge.lowering_contract: deterministic total expansion of every reachable supported HIR variant
current_mir_bridge.pair_verification: deterministically relower canonical HIR, canonicalize and verify MIR, then compare semantic bytes and provenance projection

rules.title: the bridge is preview design only
rules.contract: This contract creates no current HIR schema, MIR-X1 authority, implementation, execution, backend switch, source activation, publication, promotion, P1 closure, or product support.

rules.title: phase authority is represented by distinct types
rules.contract: HirSkeleton, AnalysisHir, TypedHirDraft, Verified<CanonicalHirH1>, ExecutableHirH1, and Verified<ProposedMirX1> are distinct authority-bearing types rather than one node graph with a phase flag.

rules.title: canonical HIR is closed
rules.contract: Canonical HIR is fully typed, resolved, responsibility-closed, canonical, and verified; recovery, unresolved, candidate, inference, error-type, missing-expression, and backend-layout variants are unrepresentable.

rules.title: static selections survive lowering
rules.contract: Selected callable, conformance, witness, requirement, extension, activation, formal, provider, actor, and authority identities remain typed fields and are never recovered from strings or runtime lookup.

rules.title: evaluation order and formal binding are distinct
rules.contract: Source operands and call inputs retain one total evaluate-once order while formal/channel binding is a separate static projection.

rules.title: power has a closed operation enum
rules.contract: Every admitted power expression seals to exactly one of CheckedIntPow, FloatPowInt, FloatPow, ComplexPowInt, ComplexPowPrincipal, or MeasurePowStatic; generic or unresolved Pow is forbidden.

rules.title: power adaptation is closed and explicit
rules.contract: Each operand uses exactly one Identity, DirectLiteralToF64Exact, F32ToF64, F32ToComplex64, or F64ToComplex64 adaptation; hidden conversion and adaptation inferred in MIR are forbidden.

rules.title: power selection is static
rules.contract: Expected result type, runtime sign, runtime integrality, Trait or witness search, provider lookup, source/import order, runtime fallback, and backend never select a power operation or result type.

rules.title: base precedes exponent exactly once
rules.contract: The base expression evaluates once, then the exponent expression evaluates once, then the sealed power operation executes once.

rules.title: math profiles are semantic dependencies
rules.contract: Every floating or complex power plan binds exact math_profile_id and special_value_profile_id values into semantic identity; host libm selection cannot replace them.

rules.title: fixed glyph conformance remains bounded
rules.contract: Stable fixed-glyph conformance remains exactly binary +, -, and *; power admits no conformance or arbitrary custom-operator route.

rules.title: Rational constants are normalized without activation
rules.contract: RationalConst refers to one reduced positive-denominator ConstRational canonical payload and creates no Rational source literal, power adaptation, Prelude identity, or runtime implementation.

rules.title: Complex constant bits preserve signed zero
rules.contract: ConstComplex64 stores ordered exact binary64 real and imaginary component bits, preserves signed zero and NaN payload bits, and selects no public layout or ABI.

rules.title: capability receipt gates executable HIR
rules.contract: Verified canonical HIR becomes ExecutableHirH1 only when exact MIR schema and lowering-rules digests cover every reachable variant with zero unsupported cases.

rules.title: capability is not execution authority
rules.contract: MirCapabilityReceipt proves bounded lowering coverage only; it does not authorize execution, implementation, MIR-X1 adoption, publication, or backend change.

rules.title: HIR to MIR is deterministic expansion
rules.contract: Lowering introduces CFG, Value, Place, token, cleanup, outcome, and suspension structure but performs no semantic lookup, conversion, fallback, or policy invention.

rules.title: pair verification relowers
rules.contract: A HIR/MIR pair is accepted only after deterministic relowering and canonical semantic-byte plus provenance comparison; matching independent digest strings are insufficient.

rules.title: MIR-X1 remains noncanonical
rules.contract: ProposedMirX1 remains a noncanonical nonactivatable compatibility target and cannot displace the current MIR semantic authority through this bridge.

rules.title: current backend authority is preserved
rules.contract: The current backend path remains xVM initial execution, LLVM AOT, and LLVM ORC JIT; neither xVM-only nor Cranelift becomes current.

rules.title: evidence boundaries remain exact
rules.contract: Semantic P0 remains zero, the exact 22 feature P1 remain OPEN, M13-A002 through A005 remain separate, every product lane remains NOT_RUN, and static fixtures are not implementation receipts.
